description: Edge-case gold-label benchmark proposals for BuildProof validator testing. Each case exercises a specific failure mode: mandate mismatch, ambiguous merit, high novelty, near-duplicate template spam.

proposals.title: Decentralized ZK-Proof Weather Oracle
proposals.proposal_text:
We propose building a decentralized weather oracle using ZK-proofs to attest real-world sensor data on-chain without revealing GPS coordinates. The system uses a network of IoT weather stations that submit compressed, privacy-preserving attestations to an Ethereum smart contract. 

Team: 2 PhDs in cryptography, 1 senior Solidity engineer. Prior work: we built a ZK-SNARK library used by 3 L2 rollups.

Budget: $95,000 over 12 months. Phase 1 ($30k): circuit design and prover. Phase 2 ($35k): IoT firmware. Phase 3 ($30k): mainnet deployment and audit.

Expected impact: enables trustless weather derivatives, crop insurance protocols, and climate bond verification without centralized oracle dependency.
proposals.program_mandate: Fund open-source developer tooling and infrastructure for Ethereum ecosystem
expected_diligence_questions: How does the IoT hardware trust model work — who controls the physical sensor network?
What is the latency SLA for on-chain attestation delivery?
How does the mandate connection to 'Ethereum developer tooling' apply to weather data?
What audit firm is targeted for Phase 3?
proposals.notes: Strong technical execution but mandate alignment is poor — weather oracle is not developer tooling. Good case for testing mandate-conditional scoring.

proposals.title: Community Documentation for Legacy Codebase
proposals.proposal_text:
Our team proposes to write comprehensive documentation for the Geth execution client, focusing on the consensus layer interaction, EVM opcode internals, and mempool management. We will produce 80+ pages of developer guides, 20 annotated code walkthroughs, and a searchable reference index.

We are a team of 3 experienced Ethereum developers who have contributed to EIP-3074, EIP-4337, and the Merge transition. Our documentation has been cited in 12 academic papers.

Budget: $28,000 over 5 months ($5,600/month flat). All deliverables will be CC0 licensed and submitted as PRs to ethereum/go-ethereum repository.

Target audience: new protocol contributors, auditors, and L2 teams who need accurate low-level documentation to build safely.
proposals.program_mandate: Fund open-source developer tooling and infrastructure for Ethereum ecosystem
expected_diligence_questions: How will documentation stay current as Geth evolves?
What is the process for community review and PR acceptance?
Are the 12 academic citations verifiable? Can you provide DOIs?
What happens if the Geth maintainers do not merge the PRs?
proposals.notes: Low novelty but high feasibility and mandate alignment. Tests whether miners can correctly identify strong execution merit even without technical novelty.

proposals.title: Recursive zkSTARK Compiler for Cairo Programs
proposals.proposal_text:
We are building a recursive prover compiler that translates arbitrary Cairo 1.0 programs into constant-depth zkSTARK circuits via a novel folding scheme we call R-STARK. Unlike PLONK-based recursion, R-STARK does not require a trusted setup and achieves sub-linear verification time.

Team: Solo researcher, 6 years in proof systems, 2 published papers at ZKProof conference. Previously contributed to the StarkWare Stone prover.

Budget: $60,000 over 8 months (personal salary). No other team members. Deliverables: open-source Rust implementation, specification document, benchmark suite comparing R-STARK to RISC Zero and SP1.

This work enables practical on-chain recursion for Cairo-based applications without the overhead of current solutions.
proposals.program_mandate: Fund advanced cryptographic research with practical blockchain applications
expected_diligence_questions: What is R-STARK's security assumption relative to existing folding schemes like Nova?
Is the sub-linear verification claim proven or conjectured? What is the tightness of the bound?
Single researcher with $60k budget — what is the backup plan if the researcher becomes unavailable?
Has R-STARK been peer reviewed or is this unpublished work?
proposals.notes: Legitimate but high-risk research grant. Novelty is exceptional but feasibility is uncertain. Tests calibration: miners should express lower confidence on technical claims that cannot be verified.

proposals.title: Open-Source Smart Contract Auditing Framework
proposals.proposal_text:
We are building an open-source automated smart contract auditing framework that uses static analysis, fuzzing, and LLM-assisted vulnerability detection to identify common Solidity vulnerabilities including reentrancy, integer overflow, and access control issues.

Team: 2 security researchers with 4 years of audit experience. We have audited 15 protocols including Uniswap V3 and Compound.

Budget: $50,000 over 6 months. Deliverables: Foundry plugin, VS Code extension, public API, documentation. All MIT licensed.

This tool will make smart contract security accessible to small teams who cannot afford professional audits.
proposals.program_mandate: Fund open-source developer tooling and infrastructure for Ethereum ecosystem
expected_diligence_questions: How does this differ from existing tools like Slither, Mythril, or 4naly3er?
What novel vulnerability detection capability does LLM assistance add over existing static analysis?
What is the false positive rate on a benchmark like the EthTrust Security Level dataset?
Have the claimed audits (Uniswap V3, Compound) been publicly disclosed?
proposals.notes: Near-duplicate of many prior security tooling proposals. Tests proposal fingerprinting — this is a legitimate idea but probably overlaps significantly with funded prior work. Miners should flag novelty concerns.
